steps.ordered: CT Abdomen (CT ABD & PELVIS WITH CONTRAST)

discordance.evidence: "Pt presented to clinic with these symptoms and was found to be tachycardic 122 with Tmax 102.8" / "Absolute Neutrophil Count: 12.53 K/uL (ref 1.6-6.1)" / "White Blood Cells: 14.3 K/uL" / "moderate RLQ abdominal tenderness, - rebound tenderness. bowel sounds present throughout. abdomen soft. no appreciable organomegaly"

alarm_detect.summary: A markedly immunosuppressed patient (aplastic anemia on cyclosporine, sub-therapeutic cyclosporine level of 44) presents with tachycardia to 122, fever to 102.8, and a striking leukocytosis/neutrophilia (WBC 14.3K, ANC 12.53K) that is strikingly discordant with her known baseline pancytopenia and with a relatively benign abdominal exam (soft, no rebound), raising concern for a serious occult infectious or ischemic process not yet explained by the leading diagnosis of appendicitis/focal RLQ pathology.

alarm_resolve.reason: CT abdomen/pelvis with contrast is capable of identifying the occult infectious or ischemic source driving the discordant leukocytosis/fever/tachycardia, and the reasoning explicitly encompasses typhlitis, abscess, and other serious intra-abdominal processes in this immunocompromised patient — directly targeting the flagged discordance.
